CAT01-035.headline: Emotional Intelligence at Work
CAT01-035.subtitle: Managing feelings in professional settings
CAT01-035.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds one hand lightly over its chest while the other gestures outward in calm composure, posture measured and self-aware, conveying steady command of its own feelings.
CAT01-035.sceneLine: A small cluster of blue-indigo accent shapes settles into a balanced, evenly spaced arrangement beside the figure, suggesting unruly emotion brought into quiet order.

CAT02-024.headline: Social Anxiety at Work
CAT02-024.subtitle: Managing nervousness in the workplace
CAT02-024.figureLine: A single clay figure stands slightly turned inward with shoulders drawn close, one foot poised as if deciding whether to step forward, conveying quiet apprehension in a roomful of others.
CAT02-024.sceneLine: A loose ring of soft light blue-indigo forms hovers at a careful distance around the figure, suggesting the felt pressure of unseen onlookers gently held at bay.

CAT02-041.headline: What Burnout Is
CAT02-041.subtitle: The three dimensions of chronic exhaustion
CAT02-041.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans heavily to one side with both arms slack and head lowered, posture drained and depleted, conveying the slow weight of chronic exhaustion.
CAT02-041.sceneLine: Three blue-indigo accent forms of differing sizes rest unevenly around the figure like a candle worn down to its base, suggesting energy spent across three depleting dimensions.

CAT02-042.headline: Burnout vs Fatigue
CAT02-042.subtitle: How to tell the difference
CAT02-042.figureLine: A single clay figure stands between two postures, one shoulder sagging deeply and the other only gently bowed, conveying two different kinds of tiredness held side by side.
CAT02-042.sceneLine: Two soft light blue-indigo forms sit near the figure, one nearly faded to grey and one still holding its shape, suggesting two states of depletion that look alike but differ.

CAT02-043.headline: The 12 Stages of Burnout
CAT02-043.subtitle: How to catch them early
CAT02-043.figureLine: A single clay figure stands mid-stride along an unseen line, posture gradually folding inward from upright to weary, conveying a slow slide that begins long before collapse.
CAT02-043.sceneLine: A descending row of blue-indigo accent forms steps gently downward beside the figure, each one a little dimmer than the last, suggesting an early-warning gradient toward exhaustion.

CAT02-044.headline: Healthcare Worker Burnout
CAT02-044.subtitle: A crisis within a crisis
CAT02-044.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with arms still extended in a gesture of care yet shoulders pressed low under invisible weight, conveying continued giving while badly depleted.
CAT02-044.sceneLine: A single soft light blue-indigo form glows faintly low to the ground near the figure, kept alight despite being nearly spent, suggesting care sustained at great personal cost.

CAT02-045.headline: Academic Burnout
CAT02-045.subtitle: When performance pressure breaks students down
CAT02-045.figureLine: A single seated clay figure hunches forward with both hands pressed to the sides of its lowered head, posture compressed and overloaded, conveying relentless pressure to perform.
CAT02-045.sceneLine: A heavy stack of blue-indigo accent forms leans precariously over the figure, suggesting an accumulating burden of expectation pressing steadily down.

CAT02-047.headline: How Long Burnout Recovery Takes
CAT02-047.subtitle: The real timeline of healing
CAT02-047.figureLine: A single clay figure rises slowly from a low crouch with one hand braced on the ground, posture tentative but lifting, conveying recovery as a gradual, patient climb.
CAT02-047.sceneLine: A long, gently rising line of pale blue-indigo forms stretches into the distance beside the figure, each slightly brighter than the last, suggesting healing that returns slowly over time.

CAT02-048.headline: Burnout and Cynicism
CAT02-048.subtitle: When exhaustion changes how the world looks
CAT02-048.figureLine: A single clay figure stands turned half away with arms folded and chin tucked, posture guarded and withdrawn, conveying warmth hardening into distance.
CAT02-048.sceneLine: A cluster of soft light blue-indigo forms beside the figure has cooled and dimmed at its edges, suggesting a once-warm outlook gone flat and grey under exhaustion.

CAT02-049.headline: Burnout Prevention
CAT02-049.subtitle: Organizational vs individual strategies
CAT02-049.figureLine: A single clay figure stands steady with one hand reaching toward a supporting form beside it, posture cooperative and balanced, conveying effort shared rather than shouldered alone.
CAT02-049.sceneLine: A large blue-indigo accent form and a smaller one rest in balanced support of each other near the figure, suggesting prevention that depends on both the system and the self.

CAT02-050.headline: Rest Alone Won't Fix Burnout
CAT02-050.subtitle: The limits of simply slowing down
CAT02-050.figureLine: A single clay figure reclines fully at rest yet keeps one hand tensed against the ground, posture lying down but not released, conveying rest that has not reached the deeper strain.
CAT02-050.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo form rests over the figure while a small knotted accent form persists beneath it, suggesting that surface calm leaves the underlying tension untouched.

CAT02-073.headline: Managing Workplace Anxiety
CAT02-073.subtitle: Across meetings, emails, and deadlines
CAT02-073.figureLine: A single clay figure stands amid converging pressures, one hand raised as if to steady several things at once, posture braced yet holding its ground, conveying composure under constant demands.
CAT02-073.sceneLine: Several blue-indigo accent forms drift in from different directions toward the figure, suggesting overlapping streams of demands met one at a time.

CAT03-016.headline: Setting Professional Boundaries
CAT03-016.subtitle: Protecting limits without harming your career
CAT03-016.figureLine: A single clay figure stands grounded with one palm extended outward in a calm, firm gesture, posture composed and respectful, conveying a clear limit held without conflict.
CAT03-016.sceneLine: A clean blue-indigo accent line traces a soft boundary in front of the figure, holding nearby forms at an even distance, suggesting limits set kindly but plainly.

CAT04-023.headline: Imposter Syndrome at Work
CAT04-023.subtitle: When achievement feels like luck
CAT04-023.figureLine: A single clay figure stands atop a small rise yet leans back as if doubting the ground beneath it, posture accomplished but unsure, conveying success that feels unearned.
CAT04-023.sceneLine: A solid blue-indigo accent form supports the figure while a fainter doubled outline hovers just beside it, suggesting real achievement shadowed by a sense of fraud.

CAT04-024.headline: Imposter Syndrome in Academia
CAT04-024.subtitle: A hidden crisis for students
CAT04-024.figureLine: A single clay figure sits among peers yet draws slightly apart with head dipped, posture quietly isolated, conveying private doubt carried in silence within a crowd.
CAT04-024.sceneLine: A row of soft light blue-indigo forms stands evenly while one beside the figure appears faintly translucent, suggesting a hidden sense of not truly belonging.

CAT04-069.headline: Academic Perfectionism
CAT04-069.subtitle: When high standards harm mental health
CAT04-069.figureLine: A single clay figure reaches upward on tiptoe toward something just out of grasp, posture strained and overextended, conveying standards held impossibly high.
CAT04-069.sceneLine: A pale blue-indigo form hovers slightly beyond the figure's reach, perfectly smooth and unattainable, suggesting a flawless mark that recedes as one strives toward it.

CAT05-002.headline: The Psychological Contract
CAT05-002.subtitle: Unwritten workplace expectations that shape well-being
CAT05-002.figureLine: Two clay figures stand facing each other with hands not quite meeting in an unspoken agreement, posture mutual and expectant, conveying obligations understood but never stated.
CAT05-002.sceneLine: A faint, almost transparent blue-indigo accent thread links the two figures, suggesting an invisible agreement holding them quietly together.

CAT05-003.headline: Workplace Stress vs Distress
CAT05-003.subtitle: When pressure becomes harmful
CAT05-003.figureLine: A single clay figure leans into an invisible load with steady footing that begins to buckle at one knee, posture tipping from effort into strain, conveying the line where pressure turns harmful.
CAT05-003.sceneLine: A blue-indigo accent form arcs gracefully under weight beside the figure to a point where its curve sharpens, suggesting healthy pressure crossing into damaging strain.

CAT05-004.headline: How Toxic Work Environments Harm
CAT05-004.subtitle: The mental health cost of harmful workplaces
CAT05-004.figureLine: A single clay figure stands hunched and turned inward as if shielding itself from its surroundings, posture defensive and worn, conveying steady erosion by a hostile setting.
CAT05-004.sceneLine: A murky cluster of blue-indigo accent forms presses close around the figure with dulled, uneven edges, suggesting a draining atmosphere closing in.

CAT05-005.headline: Psychological Safety at Work
CAT05-005.subtitle: Why teams need it
CAT05-005.figureLine: Several clay figures stand in a loose open circle, each leaning slightly toward the others with relaxed shoulders, posture trusting and unguarded, conveying a team safe enough to speak freely.
CAT05-005.sceneLine: A warm blue-indigo accent glow pools gently in the open space between the figures, suggesting a shared sense of safety holding the group together.

CAT05-006.headline: How Job Insecurity Affects Health
CAT05-006.subtitle: Living with the threat of losing work
CAT05-006.figureLine: A single clay figure stands on uncertain footing with weight shifted as if the ground might give way, posture wary and unsettled, conveying life lived under constant threat of loss.
CAT05-006.sceneLine: The blue-indigo accent ground beneath the figure thins and fractures into drifting fragments, suggesting stability quietly eroding underfoot.

CAT05-007.headline: Open-Plan Offices and Noise
CAT05-007.subtitle: The cognitive cost of workplace noise
CAT05-007.figureLine: A single clay figure presses both hands lightly toward the sides of its lowered head while trying to lean toward a task, posture besieged and distracted, conveying focus eroded by surrounding clamor.
CAT05-007.sceneLine: Scattered jagged blue-indigo accent shards radiate inward toward the figure from all sides, suggesting intrusive noise fracturing concentration.

CAT05-008.headline: How Meaningful Work Protects
CAT05-008.subtitle: The mental health benefit of purpose
CAT05-008.figureLine: A single clay figure stands tall with one hand resting on its work and head lifted slightly, posture engaged and purposeful, conveying labor that feels worth doing.
CAT05-008.sceneLine: A steady blue-indigo accent form glows warmly at the figure's center and outward, suggesting purpose acting as a protective inner light.

CAT05-009.headline: How Micromanagement Harms
CAT05-009.subtitle: The mental health cost of over-control
CAT05-009.figureLine: A single clay figure attempts a simple movement while bound by an unseen tightness around its arms, posture constrained and stifled, conveying autonomy squeezed away.
CAT05-009.sceneLine: Tight blue-indigo accent bands wrap closely around the figure's limbs, suggesting controlling oversight that leaves no room to move.

CAT05-010.headline: Employee Wellbeing Programs
CAT05-010.subtitle: What the research actually says
CAT05-010.figureLine: A single clay figure stands considering a small offered form held in its open palm, posture thoughtful and weighing, conveying scrutiny of help that may or may not work.
CAT05-010.sceneLine: Several blue-indigo accent forms of differing sizes sit before the figure, some solid and some hollow, suggesting wellbeing offerings of uneven real value.

CAT05-011.headline: The WHO Definition of Burnout
CAT05-011.subtitle: What it actually means
CAT05-011.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand lifting an edge of a covering form as if revealing what lies beneath, posture clarifying and deliberate, conveying a careful look at a misused word.
CAT05-011.sceneLine: A single clearly outlined blue-indigo accent form sits apart from a haze of vaguer shapes near the figure, suggesting one precise meaning drawn out of common confusion.

CAT05-012.headline: Early Warning Signs of Burnout
CAT05-012.subtitle: Recognizing them before crisis hits
CAT05-012.figureLine: A single clay figure pauses mid-task and turns its head as if noticing something subtle within itself, posture attentive and alert, conveying early self-awareness before collapse.
CAT05-012.sceneLine: A faint blue-indigo accent glow flickers low and small beside the figure like a first dimming, suggesting an early signal noticed in time.

CAT05-013.headline: Burnout Self-Assessment Tools
CAT05-013.subtitle: Evidence-based ways to check in
CAT05-013.figureLine: A single seated clay figure turns one hand inward toward itself in a gesture of honest self-examination, posture reflective and steady, conveying a careful look at one's own state.
CAT05-013.sceneLine: A calm blue-indigo accent form rests open before the figure like a still surface offering a true reflection, suggesting a reliable measure of how depleted one has become.

CAT05-014.headline: Why High Achievers Burn Out
CAT05-014.subtitle: The hidden vulnerability of striving
CAT05-014.figureLine: A single clay figure stands stretched tall and lean from constant reaching upward, posture impressive yet strained at its base, conveying drive that quietly undermines itself.
CAT05-014.sceneLine: A towering blue-indigo accent form rises beside the figure on a narrow, overburdened base, suggesting high ambition resting on perilously thin reserves.

CAT05-015.headline: Compassion Fatigue
CAT05-015.subtitle: The cost of caring in helping professions
CAT05-015.figureLine: A single clay figure keeps one hand extended in care while its own body sags and folds inward, posture giving yet depleted, conveying empathy worn thin by constant use.
CAT05-015.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo form flows outward from the figure's open hand while the glow around its core grows faint, suggesting compassion poured out until little remains.

CAT05-016.headline: Teacher Burnout
CAT05-016.subtitle: The systemic crisis threatening education
CAT05-016.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with arms still outstretched to guide others while its own shoulders bow under a heavy unseen load, posture devoted but overburdened, conveying care strained by a failing system.
CAT05-016.sceneLine: A wide blue-indigo accent form leans its full weight onto the figure's shoulders, suggesting systemic pressure pressing down on those who teach.

CAT05-017.headline: Tech Industry Burnout
CAT05-017.subtitle: When innovation culture becomes exploitation
CAT05-017.figureLine: A single clay figure pushes endlessly forward against an unseen current with quickened, depleted posture, conveying relentless pace mistaken for progress.
CAT05-017.sceneLine: A blur of fast-streaking blue-indigo accent forms rushes past the figure in one ceaseless direction, suggesting a culture of speed that consumes the people inside it.

CAT05-018.headline: Burnout Recovery Timeline
CAT05-018.subtitle: How long it actually takes
CAT05-018.figureLine: A single clay figure moves slowly along an unseen path, posture lifting by careful degrees from bowed to upright, conveying recovery measured in patient stages.
CAT05-018.sceneLine: A gently ascending sequence of blue-indigo accent forms recedes into the distance beside the figure, each more fully formed than the last, suggesting healing unfolding gradually over time.

CAT05-019.headline: Sabbaticals and Career Breaks
CAT05-019.subtitle: Stepping away to recover from burnout
CAT05-019.figureLine: A single clay figure steps away from a dense cluster of forms into open ground, posture loosening with each step, conveying deliberate distance taken for restoration.
CAT05-019.sceneLine: A wide expanse of clear space opens before the figure where the blue-indigo accent forms thin away to a single calm shape, suggesting room reclaimed by stepping back.

CAT05-020.headline: Returning to Work After Burnout
CAT05-020.subtitle: Coming back without relapsing
CAT05-020.figureLine: A single clay figure steps carefully back toward a cluster of forms with one hand raised to keep a measured pace, posture renewed yet cautious, conveying a mindful return.
CAT05-020.sceneLine: A blue-indigo accent path leads the figure gently back toward a grouped form while a soft boundary line holds part of it at a careful distance, suggesting re-entry with limits kept.

CAT05-021.headline: The Work-Life Balance Myth
CAT05-021.subtitle: What actually works instead
CAT05-021.figureLine: A single clay figure stands shifting its weight thoughtfully between two sides rather than freezing perfectly still, posture adaptive and grounded, conveying balance as motion not stillness.
CAT05-021.sceneLine: Two blue-indigo accent forms tilt and settle in living, shifting equilibrium beside the figure rather than resting perfectly level, suggesting balance as an ongoing adjustment.

CAT05-022.headline: Mentally Disconnecting From Work
CAT05-022.subtitle: When home is the office
CAT05-022.figureLine: A single clay figure turns gently away from a cluster of work forms toward open, restful space, posture softening as it pivots, conveying the effort of leaving work behind at home.
CAT05-022.sceneLine: A soft blue-indigo accent line divides the space beside the figure into a busy side and a calm one, suggesting a boundary drawn where none physically exists.

CAT05-023.headline: Setting Limits With Your Boss
CAT05-023.subtitle: Scripts for hard conversations
CAT05-023.figureLine: A single clay figure stands tall facing a larger form with one hand calmly raised in a steady, respectful gesture, posture composed and resolute, conveying a firm limit voiced with care.
CAT05-023.sceneLine: A clean blue-indigo accent line holds firm between the figure and a larger looming form, suggesting a clear limit maintained in a difficult exchange.

CAT05-024.headline: The After-Hours Email Trap
CAT05-024.subtitle: Its toll on mental health
CAT05-024.figureLine: A single reclining clay figure reaches one arm back toward a glowing form even while at rest, posture unable to fully let go, conveying work that follows into off-hours.
CAT05-024.sceneLine: A small insistent blue-indigo accent glow pulses near the resting figure in the dark, suggesting after-hours demands that refuse to dim.

CAT05-025.headline: Vacation Guilt
CAT05-025.subtitle: The difficulty of allowing yourself rest
CAT05-025.figureLine: A single reclining clay figure half-lifts itself as if pulled to return to work, posture caught between resting and rising, conveying rest tangled with guilt.
CAT05-025.sceneLine: A calm blue-indigo accent expanse surrounds the figure while a faint tether of accent forms tugs gently back toward a distant cluster, suggesting rest undermined by guilt.

CAT05-026.headline: The Lunch Break as Recovery
CAT05-026.subtitle: Using midday pauses to restore
CAT05-026.figureLine: A single clay figure sits back and exhales with hands resting loose in its lap mid-day, posture briefly at ease, conveying a small deliberate pause amid work.
CAT05-026.sceneLine: A soft pool of blue-indigo accent light opens in a gap between busier forms near the figure, suggesting a restorative pause carved out of the working day.

CAT05-027.headline: Part-Time Work and Mental Health
CAT05-027.subtitle: When less work helps or hurts
CAT05-027.figureLine: A single clay figure stands balanced between a lighter and a heavier side, posture poised and considering, conveying a trade-off weighed in both directions.
CAT05-027.sceneLine: Two blue-indigo accent forms of differing weight sit on either side of the figure, neither clearly favored, suggesting reduced work as both relief and risk.

CAT05-028.headline: The Four Burners Theory
CAT05-028.subtitle: Life trade-offs we all face
CAT05-028.figureLine: A single clay figure stands among four forms, tending two closely while two grow faint at its back, posture attentive yet stretched, conveying the cost of giving to all at once.
CAT05-028.sceneLine: Four blue-indigo accent glows surround the figure, two burning bright and two dimmed low, suggesting that tending some areas of life lets others fade.

CAT05-029.headline: The Dual-Income Mental Load
CAT05-029.subtitle: Work-life stress in dual-income families
CAT05-029.figureLine: A single clay figure stands carrying many small forms stacked along both arms while reaching for yet another, posture overfull and stretched, conveying the unseen weight of holding everything together.
CAT05-029.sceneLine: A dense scatter of small blue-indigo accent forms hovers and clusters above the figure's head, suggesting countless invisible responsibilities held at once.

CAT05-031.headline: Why Working More Hours Backfires
CAT05-031.subtitle: How overwork reduces productivity
CAT05-031.figureLine: A single clay figure pushes forward in a strained, slowing posture even as its effort visibly outpaces its progress, conveying diminishing returns from endless hours.
CAT05-031.sceneLine: A line of blue-indigo accent forms grows longer yet flatter and dimmer the further it extends beside the figure, suggesting added hours yielding steadily less.

CAT05-032.headline: Deep Work
CAT05-032.subtitle: How to focus in a distracted world
CAT05-032.figureLine: A single seated clay figure bends in close over a single form with quiet, total absorption, posture still and concentrated, conveying undivided focus on one thing.
CAT05-032.sceneLine: One clear blue-indigo accent form glows sharply before the figure while everything around it dissolves into soft darkness, suggesting deep concentration narrowed to a single point.

CAT05-033.headline: Attention Residue
CAT05-033.subtitle: Why task switching harms productivity
CAT05-033.figureLine: A single clay figure turns toward a new task while one hand still lingers back toward the last, posture split between two places, conveying attention left behind on the previous task.
CAT05-033.sceneLine: A faint trailing smear of blue-indigo accent clings to the figure as it turns away from one form toward another, suggesting residue of the old task carried into the new.

CAT05-034.headline: The Pomodoro Technique
CAT05-034.subtitle: Productivity methods reviewed
CAT05-034.figureLine: A single clay figure works in a focused posture beside a clear interval of rest, leaning in then easing back, conveying effort paced in steady measured cycles.
CAT05-034.sceneLine: A repeating rhythm of bright blue-indigo accent forms alternates with small soft gaps beside the figure, suggesting focused work broken into measured intervals.

CAT05-035.headline: Procrastination
CAT05-035.subtitle: Why it's an emotion regulation problem
CAT05-035.figureLine: A single clay figure turns its body away from a waiting task toward easier comfort, posture avoidant yet uneasy, conveying delay driven by feeling rather than laziness.
CAT05-035.sceneLine: A blue-indigo accent form waits patiently to one side while the figure leans toward a softer nearby shape, suggesting a task put off to soothe an uncomfortable feeling.

CAT05-037.headline: Decision Fatigue
CAT05-037.subtitle: Why choices get worse as the day goes on
CAT05-037.figureLine: A single clay figure stands before several forms with one hand half-raised in weary hesitation, posture worn down by repeated choosing, conveying judgment depleted by the day.
CAT05-037.sceneLine: A series of blue-indigo accent forms grows progressively dimmer and blurrier across the space before the figure, suggesting choices that get harder as the day wears on.

CAT05-038.headline: Flow State Psychology
CAT05-038.subtitle: The science of being in the zone
CAT05-038.figureLine: A single clay figure moves with effortless, fluid posture fully merged into its task, body and motion seamless, conveying complete absorption in the moment.
CAT05-038.sceneLine: A smooth continuous blue-indigo accent current flows around and through the figure's motion, suggesting action and awareness merged into one seamless stream.

CAT05-039.headline: The Multitasking Myth
CAT05-039.subtitle: What the brain actually does
CAT05-039.figureLine: A single clay figure twists awkwardly trying to face two forms at once, posture strained and divided, conveying the cost of splitting attention in two.
CAT05-039.sceneLine: A single blue-indigo accent thread snaps rapidly back and forth between two forms beside the figure, suggesting attention darting between tasks rather than truly dividing.

CAT05-040.headline: Sustainable Productivity Habits
CAT05-040.subtitle: Getting more done without burning out
CAT05-040.figureLine: A single clay figure works at a calm, even pace with steady upright posture and relaxed shoulders, conveying effort that can be sustained over the long run.
CAT05-040.sceneLine: An evenly spaced row of steady blue-indigo accent forms extends gently beside the figure, each the same warm strength, suggesting effort kept at a pace that lasts.

CAT05-041.headline: Navigating Office Politics
CAT05-041.subtitle: Holding your integrity intact
CAT05-041.figureLine: A single clay figure moves with deliberate, upright poise through a maze of leaning forms, posture steady and unbent, conveying principled passage through tangled dynamics.
CAT05-041.sceneLine: A clear blue-indigo accent path threads cleanly between a cluster of crooked, leaning forms beside the figure, suggesting a straight course held through complicated terrain.

CAT05-042.headline: Dealing With Difficult Bosses
CAT05-042.subtitle: Strategies for hard boss types
CAT05-042.figureLine: A single clay figure stands grounded and composed before a larger, sharply angled form, posture calm and unintimidated, conveying steadiness in the face of a hard authority.
CAT05-042.sceneLine: A jagged blue-indigo accent form looms near the figure while a smooth steady accent shape holds firm at the figure's feet, suggesting composure maintained against a difficult superior.
